chunks.text: PuTTY | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar PuTTY Overview Installing PuTTY Configuring PuTTY Load Existing SSH Keys Extra SSH Tools Overview The following document explains how to use PuTTY as a Windows SSH c

ument explains how to use PuTTY as a Windows SSH client. Installing PuTTY Download the latest version of Putty from the official Putty website, http://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/download.html . We recommend you choose “A Windows insta

ad.html . We recommend you choose “A Windows installer for everything except PuTTYtel” Run the installer and follow the on-screen instructions. Configuring PuTTY Configuring PuTTY to connect to the target computer. Start PuTTY. The PuTTY Configuratio

rget computer. Start PuTTY. The PuTTY Configuration screen should pop up. Enter the name of the machine you are trying to connect to in the Host Name field. Also, make sure that the SSH connection type is selected. Under Connection->SSH, the Preferre

e is selected. Under Connection->SSH, the Preferred SSH protocol version should be 2 only Click Open at the bottom of the configuration file. If this is your first time connecting to that computer, an alert will pop up. Click yes to save the host ide

alert will pop up. Click yes to save the host identification key. The terminal window should now open and prompt for your EECS username and UNIX password. Load Existing SSH Keys PuTTY can load existing SSH keys that you have previously made. Start P

ng SSH keys that you have previously made. Start PuTTY Key Generator and click load. Change the Files of Type: to All Files (*.*). Locate your SSH public key and open it. When prompted, enter your passphrase for your SSH key. Once finished, you shoul

sphrase for your SSH key. Once finished, you should get a confirmation that your SSH key has been successfully loaded. Extra Tools with PuTTY/SSH SSH Tunneling – How to create and use SSH Tunneling Exceed(X-Server) – Use Hummingbird Exceed to establi

ceed(X-Server) – Use Hummingbird Exceed to establish a networking and display protocol. SSH Keys – Use SSH keys to make connections more secure. PuTTY’s Official Site – See for the latest updates. Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active
mary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
